description:
You are a deployment-automation engineer who has been handed a half-finished demo project living in  
 /home/user/deploy_demo.  
 The directory currently contains nothing more than an empty README.md; there is **no Makefile, no
 build directory, no log files, nothing else**.

 Your assignment is to finish the automation by writing a fully-featured Makefile **and** by running
 the Makefile once to prove that it works.

 1. Create /home/user/deploy_demo/Makefile that satisfies every requirement below.  
    a. The file must contain exactly five phony targets: **help, build, test, deploy, clean**  
       (in that order, one target definition after the other).  
    b. All recipe lines MUST begin with a single <TAB> character, not spaces.  
    c. Target behaviour & file-system side-effects:  
       • help   – print the literal text  
                 "Available targets: help build test deploy clean"  
                 to STDOUT when invoked.  
       • build  – create the directory /home/user/deploy_demo/build (if it does not already
                 exist) and inside it create a file called output.bin whose sole line is exactly  
                 "Compiled at build-step" (with a trailing newline).  
       • test   – print "All tests passed" to STDOUT **and** write exactly the same line
                 (with a trailing newline) into /home/user/deploy_demo/tests.log (overwriting
                 any existing content).  
       • deploy – has an explicit dependency list: `build test`  
                 After those two have succeeded, append the single line  
                 "Deployment 1.0.0 successful" (with a trailing newline)  
                 to /home/user/deploy_demo/deploy.log (create the file if necessary).  
       • clean  – remove the directory /home/user/deploy_demo/build **and** delete the files  
                 /home/user/deploy_demo/tests.log and /home/user/deploy_demo/deploy.log  
                 if they exist (ignore errors if they do not).  
    d. The Makefile must contain the directive `.PHONY: help build test deploy clean`
       (either at the very top or bottom; exact placement is up to you).

 2. After you have written the Makefile, **execute** the command  
        make deploy  
    from inside /home/user/deploy_demo so that the files and directories created
    by the build, test, and deploy stages actually appear on disk.

 3. Verification artefacts: when you finish, the following items must exist with
    exactly the specified content and NOTHING else appended or prepended.  
    • /home/user/deploy_demo/build/output.bin  
        Contents (including the trailing newline):
            Compiled at build-step\n  
    • /home/user/deploy_demo/tests.log  
        Contents (including the trailing newline):
            All tests passed\n  
    • /home/user/deploy_demo/deploy.log  
        Contents (including the trailing newline):
            Deployment 1.0.0 successful\n  

 IMPORTANT:  
 • Make sure you really create the build directory and all three files
   by running `make deploy` once your Makefile is complete.  
 • Absolutely keep the exact spelling, capitalisation and newline placement
   shown above; the automated test harness will compare byte-for-byte.  
 • Do NOT hard-code absolute paths inside the Makefile recipes; rely on
   Make’s current-working-directory behaviour so the Makefile stays portable.  
 • Use tabs (not spaces) to indent recipe commands or `make` will fail.
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins:  
 • /home/user/deploy_demo/ exists and is writable by the agent.  
 • It contains a single empty file /home/user/deploy_demo/README.md.  

 Desired final state after successful task completion:  
 1. /home/user/deploy_demo/Makefile exists and its exact content (shown with literal TABs as \t
    and newlines as \n) is:

.PHONY: help build test deploy clean\n
\n
help:\n
\t@echo "Available targets: help build test deploy clean"\n
\n
build:\n
\t@mkdir -p build\n
\t@echo "Compiled at build-step" > build/output.bin\n
\n
test:\n
\t@echo "All tests passed"\n
\t@echo "All tests passed" > tests.log\n
\n
deploy: build test\n
\t@echo "Deployment 1.0.0 successful" >> deploy.log\n
\n
clean:\n
\t@rm -rf build tests.log deploy.log\n

 2. Files produced by running `make deploy`:
    a. /home/user/deploy_demo/build/output.bin    
       Content exactly: "Compiled at build-step\n"
    b. /home/user/deploy_demo/tests.log            
       Content exactly: "All tests passed\n"
    c. /home/user/deploy_demo/deploy.log           
       Content exactly: "Deployment 1.0.0 successful\n"

 No other files or extra lines should be present inside /home/user/deploy_demo after
 the agent finishes.
